name: Tadalafil BPH Randomized-Trial Safety Meta-Analysis Extract
population_or_context: Men with LUTS/BPH in randomized controlled trials of tadalafil monotherapy versus placebo.
sample_size: Eight randomized controlled trials; aggregate participant count not extracted from abstract.
methods: Meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials comparing tadalafil doses 2.5, 5, 10, and 20 mg with placebo for LUTS/BPH.
population_profile: Men with LUTS/BPH, typically older than the target healthy male 30 to 50 population; relevant to daily tadalafil exposure but indirect for off-label enhancement.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil monotherapy at 2.5, 5, 10, or 20 mg in BPH randomized trials.
indication_context: On-label/near-label BPH treatment evidence used for daily-use safety context.
safety_findings: No significant difference in serious adverse event incidence was detected after tadalafil treatment in the abstract; common adverse-event extraction was not the focus of this staged artifact.
directness_to_target_population: Moderate for daily tadalafil exposure; low for mostly healthy men age 30 to 50 because BPH populations are older and symptomatic.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Abstract-only extraction; older meta-analysis; aggregate serious-adverse-event result may mask event types, dose-duration differences, and trial exclusion criteria.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil safety or interaction evidence; transfer from other PDE5 inhibitors is not needed, but monitored trial or clinical-population context limits off-label healthy-user risk estimates.
